pr_body:
## Automated research run

**Topic:** onboard troy-piqua

Produced by `bosc research run` — read-only over the corpus. This PR adds only `data/research/` artifacts (findings + issue-proposal manifest); it alters no source bytes under `data/documents/**`. A maintainer review is required — the research App cannot approve or merge its own PR.

### Provenance
- model: `claude-opus-4-8`
- generated: 2026-06-28T18:58:40+00:00
- turns: 21 / cap 30
- tools: ToolSearch, mcp__watermark__program_overview, mcp__watermark__list_documents, mcp__watermark__list_extractions, mcp__watermark__entities, mcp__watermark__hydrology_balance, mcp__watermark__hydrology_scenario, mcp__watermark__sanitary_basis, mcp__watermark__storm_plan_inventory, mcp__watermark__timeline, mcp__watermark__retrieve_corpus
- cost: $0.8378

### Proposed follow-up issues
- 5 opened (labels: `agent-proposed`, `needs-triage`)
- 0 skipped as duplicates of open issues

See `data/research/onboard-troy-piqua-2026-06-28/findings.md` and `data/research/onboard-troy-piqua-2026-06-28/manifest.yaml`.

issues.title: Verify troy-piqua SiteProfile registration in `watermark.sites.SITES`
issues.body:
## Context
The onboarding review confirms that all MCP-accessible artifact surfaces for `troy-piqua` return empty, but the read-only BOSC tools do not expose `watermark.sites.SITES` registration or slug-scoped reach-connector outputs under `data/site/`. The findings note that epic #440 (Miami-basin expansion) lists troy-piqua as "onboarded" in the "thin sense" (profile + portable reach connectors), but this cannot be confirmed through MCP tools.

## Grounding
From the findings: *"Per the Miami-basin expansion record (epic #440), troy-piqua was 'onboarded' in the thin sense (profile + portable reach connectors). Each 'verify in producer layer' item below flags that boundary honestly rather than asserting past it."* and *"Verify the SiteProfile is registered in `watermark.sites.SITES` with `*_relpath`s, `nwis_sites`, `rsei_fips`, `eia861_utility_number`, GIS URLs. Not visible to these read-only tools — confirm in the producer layer; if absent, this precedes all of the above."*

## Acceptance Criterion
- Confirm (or create) a `watermark.sites.SITES` entry for `troy-piqua` with all required fields populated: `*_relpath`s, `nwis_sites`, `rsei_fips`, `eia861_utility_number`, and GIS URLs.
- If the entry is absent, create it and cross-reference epic #440 for intended scope.
- Document the result in a follow-up comment on this issue.

**Rationale:** Registration in `watermark.sites.SITES` is identified as a prerequisite that precedes all other onboarding work — if it is absent, no downstream artifact promotion is possible.

---
_Proposed by `bosc research run` (data/research/onboard-troy-piqua-2026-06-28); inert until a maintainer triages it._
<!-- research-dedupe: verify-troy-piqua-siteprofile-registration-in-watermark-sites-si -->

issues.title: Ingest troy-piqua source documents to bootstrap the extraction corpus
issues.body:
## Context
The onboarding review confirms that `list_documents` returns "No committed source documents for site 'troy-piqua' yet" and `list_extractions` returns "No extractions found under data/extracted/troy-piqua." The corpus index holds no troy-piqua chunks (`retrieve_corpus(site="troy-piqua")` → "No results found."). All downstream artifact surfaces (entities, timeline, program_overview, hydrology, sanitary_basis, storm_plan_inventory) are empty as a direct consequence.

## Grounding
From the findings: *"No source corpus at all. Everything downstream (entities, timeline, program overview) is empty as a consequence. Blocking at the foundational level — needs source-document ingest before any extraction work."* The Lima baseline demonstrates the expected artifact tree: recorder deeds, commissioners resolution ledger + minutes, LACRPC zoning + collection index, AEDG Tetra Tech OPC program, and a `legal/` PRR layer.

## Acceptance Criterion
- At least one source document is committed under a `troy-piqua` document tree and indexed.
- `retrieve_corpus(site="troy-piqua")` returns at least one chunk.
- `list_documents` no longer returns the empty stub for this site.
- A follow-up `retrieve_corpus` sweep is run to identify any indexed chunks that lack a `data/extracted/troy-piqua/` peer (per the process note in the findings).

**Rationale:** Without a source corpus, no extraction, entity, hypothesis, or hydrology artifact can exist — this is the single foundational blocker for all other onboarding tasks.

---
_Proposed by `bosc research run` (data/research/onboard-troy-piqua-2026-06-28); inert until a maintainer triages it._
<!-- research-dedupe: ingest-troy-piqua-source-documents-to-bootstrap-the-extraction-c -->

issues.title: Identify troy-piqua receiving-water WWTP(s), ingest OEPA NPDES fact sheet/PN, and pull ECHO DMR
issues.body:
## Context
The onboarding review confirms no OEPA NPDES fact sheets, public notices, or ECHO DMR outputs exist for `troy-piqua`. The receiving-water WWTP(s) for the Troy/Piqua reach are entirely unidentified in any committed artifact.

## Grounding
From the findings (§1): *"receiving-water WWTP NPDES ID(s) not established; no fact-sheet/PN ingest; no DMR pull. Blocking — sanitary basis and `hydrology_balance` both depend on a cited design flow + 7Q10 receptor."* The Lima baseline pattern includes `extracted/oepa/oepa-2PH00006-american-ii-fact-sheet.npdes.yaml`, `extracted/oepa/oepa-2PH00007-american-bath-fact-sheet.npdes.yaml`, and `extracted/oepa/oepa-2PK00002-shawnee-ii-draft-pn-2026-03.npdes.yaml`, plus ECHO DMR pulls regenerable via `watermark dmr <NPDES_ID> --start … --design-flow …`.

## Acceptance Criterion
- At least one Troy/Piqua area WWTP NPDES ID is identified and documented.
- Corresponding OEPA fact sheet and/or public notice is ingested as `extracted/oepa/…npdes.yaml` following the Lima naming pattern.
- An ECHO DMR connector output (`.dmr.yaml`) is committed for the identified NPDES ID(s).
- `sanitary_basis` and `hydrology_balance` surfaces are unblocked for subsequent population.

**Rationale:** The WWTP NPDES ID is a hard dependency for both the sanitary basis and the hydrology water-balance, making it a critical path item for site promotion.

---
_Proposed by `bosc research run` (data/research/onboard-troy-piqua-2026-06-28); inert until a maintainer triages it._
<!-- research-dedupe: identify-troy-piqua-receiving-water-wwtp-s-ingest-oepa-npdes-fac -->

issues.title: Configure NWIS gauge IDs, establish 7Q10, and run `hydrology_balance` for troy-piqua
issues.body:
## Context
Both `hydrology_balance` and `hydrology_scenario` return the empty `[scope]` stub for `troy-piqua`. No 7Q10 value, water-balance, or scenario is committed. It is also unconfirmed whether `nwis_sites` are configured on the SiteProfile and whether reach connectors have run.

## Grounding
From the findings (§3): *"`hydrology_balance` and `hydrology_scenario` both return the empty `[scope]` stub. No committed 7Q10, no water-balance, no scenario. `[open]` whether `nwis_sites` are configured on the profile and whether reach connectors have run (slug-scoped outputs not visible here)."* Gaps: *"confirm Great Miami / Mad River NWIS gauge IDs on the profile; establish 7Q10 for the receiving reach; run the water balance once a WWTP receptor (§1) exists. Blocking for the assimilative-screen thesis that anchors the network."* The Lima baseline uses `nwis_sites` to drive live USGS NWIS pulls and screens each WWTP design flow against a cited 7Q10.

## Acceptance Criterion
- Great Miami and/or Mad River NWIS gauge ID(s) are confirmed on the troy-piqua SiteProfile `nwis_sites` field.
- A cited 7Q10 value for the receiving reach is committed.
- `hydrology_balance` returns a populated result (not the `[scope]` stub) once the WWTP receptor from the NPDES task is available.
- This issue should be worked after the WWTP NPDES ID task is resolved.

**Rationale:** The assimilative-screen thesis — the analytical core of the BOSC network — cannot be evaluated for troy-piqua until a 7Q10 and water balance are established.

---
_Proposed by `bosc research run` (data/research/onboard-troy-piqua-2026-06-28); inert until a maintainer triages it._
<!-- research-dedupe: configure-nwis-gauge-ids-establish-7q10-and-run-hydrology-balanc -->

issues.title: Confirm troy-piqua serving utility, EIA-861 number (check short-form path), and wire OH parcel/zoning GIS for a footprint record
issues.body:
## Context
No committed grid artifact or footprint geometry exists for `troy-piqua`. It is unconfirmed whether `eia861_utility_number`, `rsei_fips`, or LMP zone are set on the SiteProfile, and no parcel/zoning extraction has been committed. Both gaps are identified as mostly or fully blocking for a complete profile.

## Grounding
From the findings (§2): *"confirm serving utility + EIA-861 number (watch the municipal EIA-861S short-form fallback — Troy and Piqua are both candidates for municipal/home-rule utilities, per the Bryan precedent); confirm PJM vs. correct RTO/LMP zone; no power basis recorded. Mostly blocking for a complete profile."* From §4: *"identify the candidate parcel assemblage; wire OH parcel/zoning GIS (OGRIP `County='Miami'`/`'Shelby'` substitute as needed); produce a footprint record (assemblage-based is acceptable, with `developed`/`impervious` `[open]`). Blocking for stormwater runoff."* The findings also flag a same-name-county hazard: *"verify situs city/state from a live sample before wiring any discovered parcel/zoning endpoint."* The Fort Wayne precedent (`idem/47378f.idem.yaml`) documents how to handle a non-disclosing permit for power basis.

## Acceptance Criterion
- The serving electric utility for Troy/Piqua is identified; the EIA-861 (or EIA-861S short-form) utility number is confirmed and set on the SiteProfile.
- The correct RTO/LMP zone (PJM or otherwise) is documented.
- OH parcel/zoning GIS endpoints (OGRIP `County='Miami'` and/or `'Shelby'`) are wired on the profile, with situs city/state verified from a live sample.
- A `bosc-site-footprint.yaml` (or equivalent) is committed with the parcel assemblage; `developed`/`impervious` may be left `[open]` following the Fort Wayne pattern.
- `hydrology_scenario` is unblocked for subsequent population once §3 is also resolved.

**Rationale:** Grid and footprint data are jointly blocking for stormwater runoff modeling and the power/cooling basis needed for the hydrology scenario, and the municipal short-form utility path requires explicit verification to avoid misidentification.

---
_Proposed by `bosc research run` (data/research/onboard-troy-piqua-2026-06-28); inert until a maintainer triages it._
<!-- research-dedupe: confirm-troy-piqua-serving-utility-eia-861-number-check-short-fo -->
